div.h2: Length-Extension Attack: Why the Length Field Is Load-Bearing

g.text: Red border = vulnerable path (no length field)
Green border = secure path (length field present)
Blue border = data structures / byte layout
RESULT: Forged MAC accepted as valid
0x00 | [0..L-1] | original message bytes (big-endian words in schedule)
0xL1 | L+1..55 | 0x00 — zero padding (k zero bytes, k >= 0)
Total: 64 bytes = 512 bits. Length always big-endian. FIPS 180-4 §5.1.1
Step 1 | ipad = 0x36 * 64 | K XOR ipad (inner key)
Step 2 | inner = SHA-256(ipad_k || msg) | inner hash
Step 3 | opad = 0x5C * 64 | K XOR opad (outer key)
Step 4 | HMAC = SHA-256(opad_k || inner) | outer hash
Security | two nested hashes | outer binds inner + key
Attack? | attacker knows inner | cannot extend: outer re-hashes with key
Inner digest | = SHA-256(ipad_k || msg) | exposed (same vulnerability)
But outer hash | SHA-256(opad_k || inner) | wraps inner with secret key
Attacker forges | needs opad_k = K XOR 0x5C | does not know K
Cannot resume | outer hash starts fresh | no state to hijack
ctx.H[0] = digest[0..3] | loaded from public digest
ctx.H[1] = digest[4..7] | loaded from public digest
ctx.H[2] = digest[8..11] | loaded from public digest
digest | encodes SHA-256(msg || pad(len)) | length is in final block
to extend | attacker needs next block after LEN | but LEN closes the block
new block | would start AFTER the length field | changes H_final
H_final | already mixed with original length | attacker cannot match

text.tspan: // Extension impossible: outer hash uses opad key
// Extension impossible: outer hash uses opad key
// Attacker loads digest into ctx.H — same as before.
// But now: the FINAL block of original message
// Appending evil_suffix requires a NEW block,
// This is NOT the same as:
// The length field in the original padding
// makes H_final depend on original length,
// so resumed compression diverges from any
// valid hash of the extended message.
